column2:
A puzzle consists of four overlapping regular hexagons. Each hexagon is made up of six triangles with a small circle marking the centre of each hexagon, as shown below. The digits 1 through 6 inclusive need to be written in the remaining empty triangles so that the sum of the digits within each hexagon is 27. No digit can appear more than once in a completed hexagon.

Identify the digit that is written in the triangles labelled A, B, and C in order to correctly
complete the puzzle.

To complete the puzzle below, four shapes—square, triangle, circle, and star—must be placed in the 4 × 4 grid so that each shape appears exactly once in each row, column, diagonal, and corner.

Identify the shape in the box labelled A and the shape in the box labelled B in order to correctly complete the puzzle.

There are three sets, [[s=300]] [A](/eɪ/) equals a set of multiples of 5, [[s=300]] B equals a set of factors of 39, [[s=300]] C equals a set of even numbers. 

Identify the two set opperations that will result in an empty set. 

A intersects B
A union C
B intersects C
B union A
A intersects C

Statistics Canada data for 2011 shows that the number of residents in Edmonton between the ages of 20 and 34 was 159445. In this age group, 133455 people had a high school diploma.

To the nearest hundredth, what is the probability that a person selected at random from this age group had a high school diploma?
0.94
0.84
0.19
0.16

A newspaper article claimed that a new medication doubled the probability of successful treatment compared to an older medication. The odds in favour of the older medication being successful in treatment were 4 colon 7.

According to the authors of the article, the odds in favour of the new medication being successful are,
8 colon 14
8 colon 11
8 colon 7
8 colon 3

The names of 25 students, including Adam and Erica, are entered into a draw for 2 different prizes. Two names will be randomly drawn without replacement to determine the winners.

Which of the following expressions can be used to determine the probability that Adam and Erica win the 2 prizes?
1 over 25 times 1 over 25
1 over 25 times 1 over 24
2 over 25 times 1 over 25
2 over 25 times 1 over 24
